what_it_does: A self-documenting Python acceptance/regression test for the `runtime/projection` instrument. It exercises `project()`, `BindingRegistry`, `separation_report()`, and `nucleation_report()` with synthetic event fixtures, asserting geometric and registry invariants across raw/grouped/time-of-day bindings, time scrubbing, address-centered radius, dyadic grid cells, registry-angle ordering, connections, two-gravity separation, and type nucleation.
observations: Starts with a long narrative docstring explaining why the file exists and warning against merging it with unrelated projection acceptance tests.
Uses global mutable `PASS`/`FAIL` counters and a hand-rolled `check()` helper instead of a test framework.
Builds synthetic events with `ev()` and checks invariants such as `r∈[0,1]`, theta inside its wedge, even `2π/n` sector widths, and no input mutation.
Tests `BindingRegistry` discovery with malformed binding files in temporary directories and expects `ValueError`.

Covers advanced modes: `now=` time scrubber, `center=` address tree-distance radius, dyadic grid cells with `rings == grid/2`, `angle_from=<registry>`, `order_by=edge`, surfaced directed sector-index edges, `whole_set`, and bidirectional edge flags.
Includes a two-gravity separator section with orthogonal blob fixtures, identical-pole and dead-pole degenerate cases, and pole-agnostic verification.

Includes a type-nucleation section with a deterministic synthetic fixture, positive control for a distinct blob, negative control for noise, dial sensitivity, dissolution candidates, bounded pile tail, and geometry projection checks.
Calls several private implementation helpers (`_grid_cell`, `_singular`, `_row_of`, `_toposort`) directly.
standouts: Locks the geometric floor (r∈[0,1], theta in wedge, even `2π/n` re-division) across every projection mode rather than freezing expected outputs.
Treats the two-gravity separator as a 'fifth gate' with explicit synthetic positive/negative controls and refuses degenerate poles.
Treats type nucleation as a non-circular control: an injected distinct blob must be born, pure noise must not.
Explicitly avoids asserting stubs that are intended to change (rings:4, radius=time) so the floor does not fight future builds.
conventions: Global mutable state for pass/fail bookkeeping.
Synthetic fixtures with deterministic seeds and explicit age/address parameters.
Extensive inline comments that narrate the design intent behind each test group.
Uses `sys.path.insert` to locate the runtime package and exits with `0`/`1` based on `FAIL`.
concerns: Single monolithic script mixing many features; no test framework means limited isolation, reporting, and fixture reuse.
Asserts against private implementation helpers (`_grid_cell`, `_singular`, `_row_of`, `_toposort`), coupling tests to internals.
Relies on magic numbers, hard-coded fixture thresholds, and exact remainder symbols like '—' that may be brittle.
Temp directories created by `discovery_raises`/`discovery_ok` are not cleaned up.
The `nucleation_report` fail-loud call passes positional arguments (`nucleation_report(_fx["item_vecs"], _fx["item_refs"], [], [], [], [])`) which may not match the expected keyword-based API.
Repeated `BindingRegistry().discover()` calls may re-scan the filesystem.
notes: A broad, narrative acceptance test that locks the projection instrument's geometric and registry invariants and exercises its advanced separator/nucleation modes with synthetic fixtures.
questions: What is the exact public signature of `project()` and how are optional arguments like `sector_ids`, `sector_edges`, `vectors`, `poles`, and `nucleation` intended to be supplied in production?
What does the `space` binding field mean for separator and nucleation modes?
How does `BindingRegistry.discover()` resolve and validate binding files, and what fields are required beyond `id`, `label`, `angle_from`, and `radius_from`?
Why does the nucleation fail-loud test call `nucleation_report` with positional arguments while other calls use keywords?
What is the intended behavior when `whole_set=True` and an event maps to a row outside the declared set?
Are the temporary directories from the discovery tests expected to be cleaned up?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the regression floor for the projection instrument, asserting only durable invariants while tolerating intentionally changing stubs; the file largely matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Data-driven, variable geometry with no hardcoded sector lists.
Fail-loud validation for malformed registry entries and missing required mode arguments.
Pure-read semantics: projection must not mutate its inputs.
Honest catch-alls and remainders so no event or row falls off the wheel silently.
Non-circular synthetic controls for advanced statistical modes.
Additive feature design where new modes do not break the base floor.
intention_signals: To encode Tim Geldard's projection equation as executable, durable invariants so that later groups (grid, time-freeze, semantic radius, registry-angle, separator, nucleation) can evolve without silently breaking the instrument's geometry or semantics.
novelty: An unusually narrative, single-file acceptance suite that deliberately refuses to freeze expected outputs, uses 'fifth gate' terminology, and provides synthetic positive/negative controls for a vector-space separator and type nucleation.
contribution: A comprehensive set of regression assertions and synthetic fixtures that define the projection instrument's expected invariants and advanced-mode behavior, preserving the design rationale in comments and providing controls a fused system would keep as a behavioral floor.
summary_for_embedding: A self-documenting Python acceptance test for `runtime/projection` that exercises `project`, `BindingRegistry`, `separation_report`, and `nucleation_report` across data-driven sectors, time/address centers, dyadic grid cells, registry-angle ordering, connections, two-gravity separation, and type nucleation using synthetic fixtures and global pass/fail checks.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the regression floor that locks the projection instrument's geometric and registry invariants while remaining tolerant of intentionally changing stubs.

symbols.does: Prints pass/fail for a named assertion and updates global PASS/FAIL counters.
symbols.description: The hand-rolled test harness primitive used throughout the file. It prints a checkmark or cross, optionally with detail, and increments the global pass or fail tally.

symbols.does: Constructs a synthetic event dictionary from seq, kind, age, and address.
symbols.description: Returns an event with a computed ISO timestamp, summary, and the provided address. It is the fixture factory for most projection tests.

symbols.does: Looks up the angular bounds of the sector containing a given point.
symbols.description: Searches the result sectors for the sector whose id matches the point's sector and returns its `[from, to]` wedge, used to verify theta lies inside the assigned sector.

symbols.does: Creates a malformed binding file and checks that discovery raises ValueError.
symbols.description: Writes the supplied content to a temporary `.py` file and calls `BindingRegistry().discover()`; returns true only if a `ValueError` is raised, used to test fail-loud registry validation.

symbols.does: Creates a well-formed binding file and checks that discovery succeeds.
symbols.description: Writes a valid `BINDING` dict to a temporary file and verifies the registry returns the binding by id, serving as the happy-path discovery check.

symbols.does: Normalizes an 8-dimensional vector to unit length.
symbols.description: Helper used in the two-gravity separator fixtures to produce unit-length pole and item vectors in an 8-D space.

symbols.does: Normalizes a vector of arbitrary dimension to unit length.
symbols.description: Helper used in the type-nucleation fixtures to normalize registry centroids, member vectors, blob vectors, and noise vectors.

symbols.does: Builds a deterministic synthetic fixture for nucleation tests.
symbols.description: Creates well-separated registered type clusters, an injected coherent blob that should nucleate, and scattered noise that should not; returns the arguments for `nucleation_report` plus reference sets for the blob and noise items.

symbols.does: Computes cosine similarity between two vectors.
symbols.description: Nested helper inside `_build_nuc_fixture` used to calculate member-to-centroid similarities when deriving per-type admission radii.
